title: descurainia incana subsp. viscosa
intro: Descurainia incana subsp. viscosa, also known as sticky whitetop or sticky false madia, is a plant species in the Brassicaceae family, native to North America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been attributed to its high content of glucosinolates. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving immune function, and potentially inhibiting the growth of certain types of cancer cells. Descurainia incana subsp. viscosa is typically prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, although its usage is largely limited to traditional and folk medicine practices.
